title: Make promptonality plugin-first across Claude and Codex, with persona observability and integration-quality evaluation
description:
## Problem

`promptonality` is intended to prove a plugin-first distribution model, but local development has drifted across multiple install surfaces.

Current failure mode:
- `plugins/promptonality/` is not being treated as the single authored source of truth everywhere
- Claude has an explicit export path, but Codex local state can silently drift
- local copied `~/.codex/skills/persona-*` directories can become stale and diverge from repo/plugin source
- the local Codex plugin cache can also drift from the repo plugin source
- this weakens the intended distribution model and makes persona behavior inconsistent across runtimes

## Why This Is P0

If the plugin is the product, distribution integrity is not optional.
A stale or split-brain install means:
- Codex and Claude may exercise different prompt surfaces
- persona behavior changes are not reliably reflected in local testing
- the repo stops being a trustworthy proving ground for the plugin distribution model
- evaluation results become noisy because they may be scoring outdated installs rather than current source

## Scope

Define and implement a plugin-first local development workflow for `promptonality` so both Claude and Codex stay aligned with current repo source.

This issue also needs to cover two evaluation tracks that become possible once distribution integrity exists:

1. Longitudinal persona observability
- observe persona speech/output behavior over time
- support weekly evaluation and iterative improvement
- verify outputs actually reflect the intended character deeply enough
- detect overuse of the same rhetorical patterns, references, cadence, or catchphrases
- distinguish shallow surface mimicry from deeper integration of the personality lens into reasoning and framing

2. On-demand integration-quality testing
- run tests on demand for quality of personality integration
- focus on integration quality, not "authenticity" as such
- score things like sentence complexity/variety, repetition, reference usage, depth of integration, and whether the persona changes framing rather than just adding a few cosmetic tics

## Acceptance Criteria

- `plugins/promptonality/` is the only authored source for plugin skills, persona packs, and manifest content
- local Codex development uses the plugin distribution path, not stale loose copied persona skills
- local Claude development also exercises the generated plugin package as part of the intended distribution story
- there is a documented sync workflow to refresh local Claude and Codex plugin installs from repo source
- there is a check/doctor workflow that fails when generated or installed artifacts drift from source
- the workflow explicitly detects and rejects stale copied `~/.codex/skills/persona-*` installs or other split-brain local states
- the evaluation plan distinguishes:
  - longitudinal persona observability over real usage/history
  - on-demand integration-quality test runs
- a weekly persona review loop is specified with concrete signals/metrics to inspect
- an on-demand integration-quality runner is specified with concrete scoring dimensions and pass/fail guidance
- docs clearly describe the plugin-first local development model and generated compatibility surfaces

## Design Notes

Plugin/distribution model:
- `plugins/promptonality/` should be the source of truth
- generated Claude-facing artifacts should be derived from plugin source, not hand-maintained forks
- Codex should exercise the plugin itself, not a parallel loose-skill shadow install
- distribution checks should compare repo source vs generated artifacts vs local installed state

Weekly persona observability ideas:
- capture or sample persona-tagged outputs over time for review
- generate weekly summary reports per persona or per workflow/persona pair
- track repeated phrase / catchphrase frequency
- track repeated reference-template reuse
- track sentence-length and sentence-shape variety
- track whether persona affects reasoning structure, tradeoff framing, and emphasis ordering, not just vocabulary
- track reference density and whether references are contextually earned vs sprayed mechanically
- score "depth of integration" separately from "recognizability"
- include regression checks for flattening into generic assistant voice

On-demand integration-quality testing ideas:
- fixed prompt suite across personas and neutral baselines
- score repetition, lexical variety, sentence complexity, rhetorical variety, and reference usage
- detect obvious template reuse and overfitting to signature phrases
- verify persona changes framing/analysis priorities, not just surface wording
- keep this separate from canonical-character authenticity judgments
- support quick local runs plus richer offline review/report output

Potential outputs/artifacts:
- sync script / doctor command for promptonality local development
- weekly evaluation report format
- integration-quality test runner and scoring rubric
- documentation for how to review and improve persona packs based on evidence
design: Single source of truth should be plugins/promptonality/. Generated Claude artifacts must derive from plugin source. Codex must exercise the plugin distribution path rather than stale loose persona skill copies. Evaluation must separate longitudinal observability from on-demand integration-quality scoring.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin-first local development workflow exists; local Claude/Codex installs can be refreshed and checked from source; weekly persona observability plan is defined; on-demand integration-quality testing plan is defined.
notes: Triggered by discovery that local Codex persona skills and local plugin cache can drift from repo source, including stale persona files and outdated wrapper-era artifacts.

comments.author: Ivan Cherniukh
comments.text: Completed the install/docs slice: updated root packaging docs to treat plugins/promptonality as the repo-local Codex plugin source, rewrote the promptonality README around dynamic composition, regenerated Claude-facing exports, and fixed the Claude plugin export check to ignore __pycache__/pyc artifacts. Remaining epic work still includes local sync/doctor workflows and the observability/integration-quality evaluation tracks.

title: Audit and redesign Helix keybindings for intuitive expansion
description: Review the current Helix shortcut layout, identify collisions and underused prefixes, work through the workflows I actually need day to day, and redesign the keybinding structure so related actions are grouped intuitively and can grow cleanly over time. Scope should include: current-state audit, missing workflow inventory, leader/prefix strategy, shell/LLM/editor command grouping, and a migration plan that avoids turning the config into an ad-hoc pile of binds.

title: Add on-demand integration-quality testing for promptonality personas
description:
## Problem

The epic calls for on-demand integration-quality testing, but there is no concrete runner or rubric yet for evaluating how well a personality is integrated into output quality.

This is separate from authenticity or canonical fidelity. The problem here is quality of integration: whether the persona changes framing, variety, and reasoning in a non-mechanical way.

## Scope

Design and implement an on-demand integration-quality test path for `promptonality`.

The test path should evaluate dimensions such as:
- lexical and rhetorical variety
- sentence complexity and shape variety
- repetition and template reuse
- reference usage quality
- whether persona changes analysis priorities and framing
- whether the persona remains useful rather than collapsing into decorative noise

## Acceptance Criteria

- define a fixed prompt suite or equivalent evaluation input set
- define scoring dimensions for integration quality
- include repetition detection and obvious-template-reuse detection
- include a signal for framing/analysis changes vs surface wording only
- support local on-demand execution
- document expected outputs and pass/fail interpretation

## Notes

Keep this separate from authenticity judgments about how closely a persona matches canon.
design: Measure integration quality rather than authenticity: repetition, variety, reference usage, framing changes, and usefulness under persona overlay.
acceptance_criteria: An on-demand local evaluation path exists with defined prompts, scoring dimensions, and pass/fail interpretation for persona integration quality.

title: Fix shallow caricatured persona application and catchphrase overuse
description:
## Problem

Recent session feedback exposed a concrete persona-integration defect: the persona application fell into shallow, repetitive surface markers and read as fake or caricatured rather than well-integrated.

Specific failure pattern to investigate:
- overuse of signature catchphrases and profanity
- low situational modulation
- shallow performance that feels forced rather than character-grounded
- insufficient adaptation to user feedback in the moment
- low linguistic variety, creating a blunt caveman-like effect instead of a deeper persona lens

## Scope

Investigate and fix the failure mode where a persona is technically recognizable but integrated poorly.

The goal is not to mute persona entirely. The goal is to avoid shallow imitation and make persona expression more context-sensitive, varied, and structurally integrated into reasoning.

## Acceptance Criteria

- document the failure mode and why it happened
- identify which parts belong to pack content vs application logic vs evaluation gaps
- define safeguards against repetitive catchphrase spam and surface-only mimicry
- define how user correction should immediately modulate persona intensity and delivery
- add this defect class to the integration-quality evaluation plan
- document expected behavior for recognizable but non-caricatured persona delivery

## Notes

User feedback to preserve: the current behavior sometimes reads like a caveman faking the persona rather than actually embodying it well.
design: Treat this as a persona-integration bug: separate pack-content issues from application-policy issues and add protection against repetitive surface-only mimicry.
acceptance_criteria: Failure mode is documented, root causes are identified, safeguards are specified, and the defect class is covered by the evaluation plan.

title: Add weekly persona observability workflow for promptonality
description:
## Problem

The epic calls for weekly persona observability, but there is no concrete workflow yet for sampling outputs, summarizing behavior over time, or deciding when a persona pack needs adjustment.

Without a recurring review loop, persona changes are hard to evaluate longitudinally and regressions can hide behind anecdotal impressions.

## Scope

Design and implement a weekly persona observability workflow for `promptonality`.

The workflow should answer questions like:
- does a persona remain recognizably aligned over time
- is the persona integrated into framing and reasoning, not just surface wording
- is the style flattening into generic assistant voice
- is the persona overusing the same references, turns of phrase, cadence, or sentence shapes
- are certain workflows/personas degrading faster than others

## Acceptance Criteria

- define what outputs/history are sampled and how often
- produce a weekly report format with concrete signals to inspect
- include repetition and phrase-frequency analysis
- include sentence-shape / length-variety analysis
- include reference-usage density and contextual-fit analysis
- include a depth-of-integration signal distinct from raw recognizability
- define how findings feed back into pack or workflow revisions
- document the workflow in the repo

## Notes

This should evaluate actual longitudinal behavior, not just isolated test prompts.
design: Focus on longitudinal evidence: repetition, sentence variety, reference use, generic-voice regression, and depth-of-integration signals over time.
acceptance_criteria: Weekly report format, sampling plan, concrete observability signals, and pack-improvement feedback loop are defined and documented.

title: Implement promptonality sync and evaluation follow-ups
description: Finish the remaining epic scope after the installation/docs alignment: add a local sync or doctor workflow for plugin-vs-generated-vs-installed drift, define the weekly persona observability loop, and add an on-demand integration-quality runner or rubric for persona evaluation.

title: Align promptonality installation docs and export checks
description: Update promptonality installation surfaces to reflect the plugin-first model, regenerate Claude-facing artifacts from plugin source, and make export/smoke checks pass against the current dynamic-composition design.

comments.author: Ivan Cherniukh
comments.text: Almost ready, but not yet ready to close. The promptonality installation/docs and export-check alignment work is in place locally, but it has not been fully tested or reviewed, and git wrap-up is still pending.

title: Migrate skills/ and agents/ to symlinks via link-agent-assets.sh
description:
The script scripts/link-agent-assets.sh already supports --force to replace real dirs with symlinks (with .bak backup). Need to run it with --apply --force --claude-only to convert all 12 installed skill dirs and agent files to symlinks pointing to this repo.

After applying:
- Edit skills/agents in this repo only — changes are live immediately in ~/.claude/
- No more manual sync or duplicate edits needed

Command to run when ready:
  bash scripts/link-agent-assets.sh --claude-only --force --apply

Then verify with:
  ls -la ~/.claude/skills/

Then delete the .bak.* dirs once confirmed.

title: Add global pi /exit alias
description: Add a user-level pi extension that registers /exit as a graceful shutdown alias for /quit.

title: Research Helix-to-LLM inline workflow for plz alias
description: Investigate a richer editor-driven workflow beyond the first shell-wrapper version: Helix selection piped to a global alias, replacement vs scratch-buffer behavior, floating or side-pane UI options, Codex vs Claude routing, and whether zellij panes/popups can provide a better interaction surface for iterative prompting.

comments.author: Ivan Cherniukh
comments.text: Additional direction from follow-up exploration: investigate a sidecar Codex/Claude feedback surface for Helix that can show brief reasoning or progress without replacing the selection output. Candidate surfaces: 1) external pane or popup via zellij, 2) terminal-side floating window if the multiplexer supports it, 3) lightweight status-bar or append-output path inside Helix, 4) split scratch buffer for explanations/logs while keeping :pipe replacement clean. The useful distinction is likely: replacement output goes back into the buffer, while thought/progress goes to a side channel.

title: Add context-window-inspector to catalog and test installation
Add skills manifest (skills.yaml) per config set for third-party skill sourcing
description:
Design: borrow lagz0ne/local-install-skill's .claude/local-plugins.yaml pattern.

Each config set (configs/baseline/, configs/experimental/, etc.) gets a skills.yaml declaring what skills it needs and from where. scripts/deploy.sh reads this and calls 'npx skills add' for each entry.

Proposed format:
  skills:
    user:                          # installs to ~/.claude/skills/
      - source: local/beads        # this repo
      - source: anthropics/skills/pdf   # GitHub via npx skills
    project:                       # installs to .claude/skills/ in target project
      - source: wshobson/agents/scout

Integration:
  - deploy.sh iterates skills.yaml entries
  - user-scope entries: npx skills add -g <source>
  - project-scope entries: npx skills add <source> (run from target project dir)
  - local entries: handled by link-agent-assets.sh (symlink, not copy)

Reference: lagz0ne/local-install-skill (.claude/local-plugins.yaml)
Reference: vercel-labs/skills (npx skills, 11.9k stars) — the install primitive to wrap

title: Normalize and place tmux dev layout helper
description: Replace the stray tmux-layout-test.sh root script with a generic, robust helper under scripts/. The helper should support configurable session name, root, optional pane commands, and correct attach/switch behavior without assuming a specific project like multimeter.

value: Promptonality debugging: direct Jesse packet injection into the Responses API showed the Jesse pack itself was strong from line one; weak persona behavior in normal runtime pointed to persona-apply/persona-start not explicitly requiring the selected pack YAML to be read before activation. Added mandatory pack-load rules to both skills and regenerated repo plugin artifacts.
User preference: never use the words 'scaffold' or 'harness' in responses. Prefer 'boilerplate', 'starter layout', 'test script', or 'test runner'.

Promptonality install state: repo plugin sources and generated Claude plugin package were updated, but live runtime installation was done incorrectly via standalone skill directory copies into ~/.codex/skills and ~/.claude/skills. User clarified persona skills should be installed only via plugin; actual plugin installation into Codex/Claude runtime locations still needs to be handled in a future session.

DEFERRED IDEA - Idea 1: CC Tool Discovery/Recommendation Agent. A command or subagent that takes a natural language question ('how do I do X in Claude Code?'), scans installed tools/skills/plugins/agents, and recommends the right approach. Like a smart find-skills that knows what's actually installed. Does NOT invoke — only recommends. Separate from Idea 2 (Kim orchestrator).
